description: Explicit decisions for specialized theme near-color pairs. Keep this file in sync with scripts/audit-theme-colors.mjs output; lower the audit baseline when a pair is merged.

decisions.reason: Tokyo Night primary background uses the canonical Tokyo base hue; #1c1c1f is the default dark/china/slate neutral surface. Merging would flatten cross-theme identity rather than remove an app-level duplicate.
decisions.reevaluateWhen: Only after a visual review proves Tokyo Night no longer needs a distinct primary background identity.

decisions.reason: China Night secondary background keeps a warmer ink tint next to the shared dark neutral; it is a theme-identity surface, not a stray duplicate.
decisions.reevaluateWhen: Only if China Night background surfaces are redesigned around the shared neutral scale.

decisions.reason: Light quaternary surface and slate button text sit near primary text/background stops; they can appear as adjacent surface/text roles and should not be collapsed without focused screenshots.
decisions.reevaluateWhen: Only after button text and light surface-ramp screenshots show no loss of hierarchy.

decisions.reason: China Style elevated paper keeps a warm paper tint while #e8e8e8 is a shared cool neutral/text stop. The distinction is a theme personality cue.
decisions.reevaluateWhen: Only if China Style paper surfaces are intentionally moved to the shared neutral ramp.

decisions.reason: The pair crosses light primary background and Monaco lineHighlight / button state values. Editor line highlight must remain visibly distinct from base surface roles.
decisions.reevaluateWhen: Only after Monaco-focused light-theme screenshots confirm the line highlight remains readable.

decisions.reason: Midnight background, tertiary surface, and editor lineHighlight are adjacent in the same dark theme. Merging would weaken surface and editor-row separation.
decisions.reevaluateWhen: Only after a Midnight surface-ramp redesign removes the adjacent role split.

decisions.reason: China Style paper uses a warm first-paint surface while #f3f3f5 is a cool light neutral and button state stop. This is cross-theme identity, not a duplicate role.
decisions.reevaluateWhen: Only after China Style paper identity is intentionally neutralized.

decisions.reason: Light strong text and Tokyo border encode different theme-local roles. They are not the same semantic token even though their RGB distance is low.
decisions.reevaluateWhen: Only if both roles are remapped through a shared semantic border/text projection.

decisions.reason: China Style tertiary and elevated paper surfaces are adjacent paper-ramp stops. Collapsing them would reduce section/elevation separation.
decisions.reevaluateWhen: Only after China Style paper-ramp screenshots prove the two stops are not adjacent in product UI.

decisions.reason: The 0.12 and 0.15 white overlay stops separate hover/active/elevated states and must stay aligned with static tokens.
decisions.reevaluateWhen: Only after the runtime and static overlay ladder are simplified together with visual coverage for affected card, markdown, and shadow states.

decisions.reason: The 0.12 and 0.15 black overlay stops separate light-theme card/elevated/active states and must stay aligned with static tokens.
decisions.reevaluateWhen: Only after light-theme surface-state screenshots show no hierarchy loss.

decisions.reason: Static token stops mirror runtime overlay state levels for first paint. Removing one side without runtime alignment would create early-render drift.
decisions.reevaluateWhen: Only when the matching runtime overlay pair is also retired.

decisions.reason: Static black overlay stops mirror runtime light-theme state levels for first paint. The pair is a state ramp, not an unowned duplicate.
decisions.reevaluateWhen: Only when the matching runtime overlay pair is also retired.

decisions.reason: Cyber and default dark installer cards keep distinct theme preview backgrounds; the values appear as separate theme cards rather than adjacent layers inside one installer surface.
decisions.reevaluateWhen: Only after installer theme preview screenshots show Cyber and default dark cards no longer need separate preview identity.

decisions.reason: Tokyo Night primary background intentionally remains close to the web Tokyo hue while the shared installer card surface stays neutral for preview contrast.
decisions.reevaluateWhen: Only if the installer Tokyo Night preview is redesigned to inherit the shared dark card surface.

decisions.reason: Ink Night keeps a warm dark preview background near the default dark installer card background; this communicates theme identity across separate theme cards.
decisions.reevaluateWhen: Only after installer preview screenshots prove the warm Ink Night identity is no longer visually meaningful.
